Every Sunday night, this workflow fetches all Klaviyo campaigns to calculate weekly performance metrics. It then uses AI to analyze whether email open rates have dropped week-over-week. If open rates have declined, the workflow maps the nearest coworking space to help plan an urgent strategy session. If open rates are stable or improved, the workflow ends without further action.
Retrieved 12 campaigns from the past week with metrics: Campaign A (open_rate: 22.5%), Campaign B (open_rate: 18.3%), Campaign C (open_rate: 24.1%), Campaign D (open_rate: 19.8%)
Retrieved previous week's 11 campaigns with metrics: Previous avg open_rate: 23.4%, current week avg: 21.175% - showing a 2.2% week-over-week decline
Found nearest coworking spaces: 1) TechHub Downtown (0.8 miles, open now), 2) Workspace Plus (1.2 miles, open now), 3) Office Collective (1.5 miles, closes in 2 hours)

Every day at 9am, the workflow checks the current weather forecast using the built-in GF_WEATHER tool. If rain is forecast, it then searches Salesforce for any outdoor client meetings scheduled for the day. If rain is detected, a Telegram message is sent to remind you to reschedule any outdoor Salesforce client meetings.
Rain forecast: 80% precipitation expected this afternoon
Found 3 opportunities with outdoor client meetings scheduled for today

When a new Shopify order is placed, the workflow checks if the order total exceeds $200. If it does, the customer's details are retrieved from Shopify, then their information is looked up in Mailchimp and they are added to a VIP audience segment in Mailchimp. If the order is $200 or under, no action is taken.
